shellSubtitle: Core access and leaderboard guard check
debugSubtitle: Operate and verify guard, cache, and rate-limit behavior

profile.title: Physical profile
profile.subtitle: Used as the reference for body-weight and age-based scoring. Stored on this device (Core, no account required).
profile.completeBadge: Baseline complete — ready for assessment inputs that use height, weight, age, and gender.
profile.incompleteHint: Add your body data so later assessments can convert real metrics to scores.

errors.invalid-gender: Gender must be male or female.
errors.invalid-height: Height must be between 120 and 230 cm.
errors.invalid-weight: Weight must be between 35 and 250 kg.

home.overallFormula: Sum of six core scores ÷ 6 (arm circumference excluded).

ladder.title: Live ladder
ladder.body: You have Core + Pro access. Rankings load through the leaderboard service.
ladder.bodyRemote: Connected to Firestore — summary rows only, with client cache + best-score writes.
ladder.bodyLocalMock: Firebase env not set — showing in-memory mock data for development.
ladder.noNetworkNote: Non-Pro accounts never hit Firestore from this route.
ladder.cacheHit: Serving from local session cache (TTL applies).
ladder.loadError: Could not load rankings. Check Firebase rules and indexes.
ladder.empty: No pilots on this shard yet — be the first upload.

community.title: Community uplink
community.subtitle: Squads, challenges, and shared missions will dock here — the route is live, feature modules ship next.
community.card1Body: Future: crew channels, shoutouts, and spotlight runs without touching your local training data.
community.card2Body: Tie-ins to ladders stay Pro-gated — Community itself never phones home until you explicitly share.

tools.title: Tools & cloud sync
tools.subtitle: Assist systems for backup, locale, and future calculators — minimal, fast, no noise.
tools.consoleBody: HUD calibration, latency probes, and dev panels will anchor in this slot. Local-first stays default.
tools.languageHint: Flip between English and Traditional Chinese for field tests.
tools.syncBody: Pro subscribers can snapshot scores + local history to Firestore (single document, JSON payload).
tools.syncProOnly: Cloud sync requires Pro — open Join Arena if you need the uplink.

sync.backupOk: Backup written to your Pro cloud slot.
sync.backupFail: Backup unavailable (check Pro + Firebase + anonymous auth).
sync.restoreOk: Local scores and history replaced from cloud snapshot.
sync.restoreFail: Restore failed — verify Pro, Firebase config, and network.

history.title: Assessment history
history.subtitle: Snapshots saved from Assessment (device-only, Core — no subscription required).
history.empty: No snapshots yet. Save one from the Assessment tab.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.subtitle: Enter raw scores per axis (0–200; typical manual entry stays 0–100; body-fat may exceed 100 after FFMI). Totals update on Home (linear average ÷ 6).
assessment.localFirstNote: Core-only: scores stay on device — no Firebase on this screen.
assessment.homeRadarHint: Radar and overall average are shown on Home.

ffmi.title: FFMI (body-fat axis)
ffmi.body: Compute FFMI from Home profile + body fat %. Radar accepts the score only while adjusted FFMI is at or below the world-record anchor for your sex.

cardio.title: Cardio (Cooper / 5 km)
cardio.body: 12-minute Cooper test with age- and sex-based standards, or 5 km time (20 min → 100+; 45 min → 0). Raw inputs are stored locally; the radar prefers Cooper when a valid distance is saved.

cardio.title: Cardio assessment
cardio.subtitle: Cooper 12-minute run (meters) or 5 km time. Cooper needs a complete Home profile; 5 km time only needs your finish time.
cardio.cooperHint: Run on a track or measure with GPS. Scoring uses the same age/sex tables as the reference app.

cooperInfo.title: Cooper 12-minute run test
cooperInfo.p1: The 12-minute run field test is widely credited to Dr. Kenneth H. Cooper, former U.S. Air Force colonel, as a practical aerobic screening tool. Gold-standard VO₂ max is usually measured in a lab under maximal effort, which is hard to scale; the Cooper protocol uses maximal distance covered in 12 minutes as a field estimate of cardiorespiratory fitness / VO₂ max—and it has roots in military fitness screening. (Educational/fitness context only—not a medical diagnosis.)
cooperInfo.p2: How to perform: use a track or another safe, flat route; record total meters via lap counting or a sports watch / GPS; do a 10–15 minute dynamic warm-up beforehand to reduce injury risk.
cooperInfo.p3: Your app score maps sex and age to a norm distance table: 60-point and 100-point anchor distances differ by age band, so the same distance can yield very different scores. We linearly interpolate between the 60- and 100-point anchors and allow scores above 100 beyond the top anchor. This app provides a Cooper age band from 13+.
cooperInfo.p4: Norm distances follow the same lineage as commonly published Cooper test charts (including references to chart compendia such as the Cooper Test Chart) and secondary citations of chart work associated with Carl Magnus Swahn. Institutions may use slightly different bands; this app interpolates using the same anchor table as our fitness reference implementation.

cardio.run5kmHint: Faster than 20:00 scores above 100; 45:00 or slower is 0. Times are stored on device only.
cardio.submitDone: Saved — Home radar and history can use this score.
cardio.profileIncompleteHint: Cooper needs age and sex from your Home profile. You can still use the 5 km tab with time only.

errors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home to use Cooper.
errors.missing-profile-cooper: Set age and sex on Home before running Cooper.
errors.invalid-cooper-distance: Enter a positive distance in meters.
errors.cooper-no-standard: No Cooper table for this age range — check the age on your profile (13+).
errors.invalid-5km-time: Enter a valid 5 km time (minutes and/or seconds).

ffmi.title: FFMI assessment
ffmi.subtitle: Fat-free mass index from your saved physical profile plus body-fat %. If adjusted FFMI exceeds the documented world-record anchor for your sex, saving to the radar is blocked until you adjust inputs — future versions may add an overclock visualization.
ffmi.bodyFatHint: Reasonable inputs are about 3–60%. Uses height, weight, and gender from Home.
ffmi.worldRecordLockIntro: Your adjusted FFMI is above the documented elite-record anchor for your sex. The radar cannot plot this run yet — change body fat or profile data until you are at or below the limit. A dedicated overclock mode for the chart is planned.
ffmi.submitRadar: Write score to radar (body fat axis)
ffmi.submitDone: Stored locally — radar on Home updates.

errors.missing-profile: Physical profile incomplete — add gender, age, height, and weight on Home first.
errors.invalid-body-fat: Body fat must be between 3 and 60%.
errors.world-record-lock: Adjusted FFMI is above the world-record anchor — adjust body fat or profile before writing to the radar.

info.whatIsIntro: FFMI (Fat-Free Mass Index) reflects lean mass versus height and body fat — often more informative than BMI alone for training context. Higher values generally mean more fat-free mass among people with similar height. Results can skew when:

caveats.tall: Height is far above average (around ≥190 cm). This app applies a tall correction above 1.8 m.
caveats.highFat: Body fat percentage is unusually high relative to typical reporting ranges.
caveats.heavy: Body weight is unusually high for height, which affects estimated fat-free mass.

table.sectionHint: Bands are educational labels only — not a medical or PED diagnosis.
